blurb:
His name is Hendrix, and yes, the personality matches. He's a two-year-old pit bull mix weighing in at 63 pounds of goofy, playful energy, and the shelter says he loves to play and needs a family who can keep up with him. If you've been looking for a dog who will actually motivate you to get outside and move, this might be your guy.

He does play rough with other dogs, so he's probably best with a dog who can match his energy rather than one who'd get steamrolled. Housetrained status is unknown since he came in without much history, but at two years old he's got the basics of a grown dog without the full senior-dog slowdown. He's got room to settle into a routine.

One thing to know upfront: Hendrix tested positive for heartworms. That sounds scarier than it is. Lewisville Animal Services covers his full treatment as part of the adoption, and heartworm disease is very manageable with the right protocol. Call the shelter at 972-219-3478 if you want the details before you come in. He's vaccinated, microchipped, neutered, and ready to go once treatment wraps. Walk-ins welcome during visiting hours at the College Pkwy location in Lewisville.
shelter_hours: Contact shelter for current visiting hours
scoring_notes:
• Heartworm-positive status covered fully by the shelter is a powerful myth-busting hook that rewards newsletter education
• Pit bull mix with unknown house training and rough play style narrows fit but the name and personality make him memorable
• High shelter time score reflects heartworm diagnosis and breed bias as compounding adoption barriers driving urgency

blurb:
Squash is a two-and-a-half-year-old boxer mix, 57 pounds, with a moderate activity level and a face that will absolutely make you stop scrolling. If you've spent any time around boxers, you already know the deal: goofy, loyal, and deeply convinced that they belong on the couch with you.

He gets along with other dogs, which makes him a solid candidate for a multi-dog household or someone who wants to do dog park weekends. His history on kids and house training is unknown since his background is thin, but at his age and size, he's past the chaotic puppy phase and likely to settle into a household routine without much drama. Boxers tend to be people-oriented dogs, and that usually makes the transition easier.

Squash is fully vaccinated, heartworm negative, microchipped, neutered, and flea-treated. Clean bill of health across the board. You can meet him by walking in during visiting hours at Lewisville Animal Services on College Pkwy. Call ahead at 972-219-3478 if you have questions before you come.
shelter_hours: Contact shelter for current visiting hours
scoring_notes:
• Clean bill of health and heartworm-negative status make him the lowest-barrier adoption in the dog group
• Boxer personality archetype—goofy, couch-loving, people-oriented—writes itself and connects instantly with a broad readership
• Lowest ID number in the entire candidate pool strongly implies the longest shelter stay, making adoption urgency the lead angle

blurb:
Lefty is a one-year-old shepherd mix, almost 80 pounds, and still in full puppy-brain mode at 16 months. His activity level is listed as high, which is shelter-speak for: he needs a person who actually wants to do something with their dog. Walks, fetch, a yard to burn through, whatever. He's got the energy for all of it.

He's already housetrained, which is a genuinely big deal for a dog his age who ended up in a shelter. He gets along with other dogs, though he likes to take it slow on introductions rather than diving headfirst into a dog park situation. Patient meet-and-greet, not chaos. That's actually a good sign for trainability.

He did test positive for heartworms, and Lewisville Animal Services covers his full treatment as part of the adoption. Heartworm is very treatable, especially caught early, and the shelter will walk you through the protocol if you call 972-219-3478 before coming in. He's vaccinated, microchipped, neutered, and flea-treated. Young, housetrained, social with dogs, and the shelter is covering his medical care. For a big shepherd mix at this age, that's a strong package. Stop by College Pkwy in Lewisville during visiting hours to meet him.
shelter_hours: Contact shelter for current visiting hours
scoring_notes:
• Already housetrained at 16 months in a shelter setting is a standout credential that signals trainability and routine readiness
• Shelter-covered heartworm treatment removes the biggest adoption hesitation and makes the value proposition unusually strong
• Lower ID number relative to peers suggests the longest shelter stay in the dog group, giving this feature real urgency

blurb:
Luna has lived with kids, adults, seniors, dogs, and cats, which is a pretty impressive resume for a one-year-old. She's done it all already and handled all of it. The shelter describes her as independent, and she did take some time to settle in after arriving, but that tracks for a cat who suddenly went from a busy household to a shelter environment.

She weighs about 8 pounds, she's litter box trained, and she's fully vaccinated, spayed, and microchipped. If you have a full house, Luna has already proven she can hang. If you want a cat who won't be velcroed to you 24 hours a day but will share your space on her own terms, she might be exactly right.

She's at Lewisville Animal Services on College Pkwy. Walk in during visiting hours to meet her.
shelter_hours: Visit during shelter visiting hours (call to confirm current schedule)
scoring_notes:
• Proven compatibility with kids, seniors, dogs, and cats makes her the broadest-fit candidate in the pool
• Young, fully vetted, and low-maintenance personality appeals to busy households and first-time cat owners
• Backstory is thin and shelter time appears short, limiting urgency and emotional hook for readers

blurb:
Rudy is a year and a half old and still very much figuring out the whole human thing. He hasn't warmed up to being petted yet, but he loves to play, and that's actually a great starting point. Cats like Rudy who are toy-obsessed will usually come around on their own timeline once they feel safe and settled.

He weighs 10 pounds, he's litter box trained, and he gets along fine with other cats. He's fully vaccinated, neutered, and microchipped. The shelter is looking for someone with patience and space to let him adjust at his own pace. If you're not in a rush for a lap cat and you like the idea of earning a cat's trust over time, Rudy has a lot of potential. He does best without young children in the home.

He's at Lewisville Animal Services on College Pkwy. Stop by during visiting hours to meet him.
shelter_hours: Visit during shelter visiting hours (call to confirm current schedule)
scoring_notes:
• The earn-a-cat's-trust narrative is a strong emotional hook for experienced cat owners who read newsletters closely
• No-young-children restriction and shy temperament narrow the adopter pool considerably, raising urgency
• Longer implied shelter stay due to selectivity requirements makes him a compelling underdog feature candidate

blurb:
Eight years on the street will give a cat some character, and Pacino has plenty of it. He's done the outdoor life, figured out how to handle himself, and has now decided he's completely done roughing it. He's ready to be inside, warm, and thoroughly spoiled. The shelter describes him as a lover boy with a gentle meow, and the tough-guy name is a little misleading once you meet him.

He's litter box trained, fully vaccinated, neutered, and microchipped. They don't know yet how he does with other pets or kids since his background is a bit of a mystery, but the shelter can help set up a meet and greet if that's a concern.

Pacino is at Lewisville Animal Services on College Pkwy. Stop by during visiting hours to meet him in person. A cat who's survived eight years outside and still turned out this sweet is not going to last long.
shelter_hours: Visit during shelter visiting hours (call to confirm current schedule)
scoring_notes:
• Eight years as a street survivor turned gentle lover boy is a headline-ready redemption arc
• Fully vetted and litter box trained with a charming personality that defies his tough-guy name
• Unknown pet/kid compatibility adds friction but shelter meet-and-greet option softens that barrier
